clusters.title: US strikes Iranian missile sites as peace talks continue
clusters.neutral_headline: US launches new strikes on Iran, targeting missile sites and boats
bullet_summary: US forces struck missile sites and vessels allegedly laying naval mines in southern Iran late on Monday, the United States Central Command said
The strikes, which the Pentagon said were taken "in self-defence", threaten to further destabilise a fragile ceasefire as Iranian negotiators are in Doha for high-stakes talks aimed at ending the war
In turn, Hezbollah said it staged several attacks on Monday on three barracks and a military post in northern Israel “in response to the violation of the ceasefire” by Israel
military struck targets in Iran near the Strait of Hormuz in what a spokesman for the military said Monday were acts of self-defense

items.title: US strikes Iranian missile sites as peace talks continue
items.standfirst: Washington announces ‘self-defence’ operation against targets in southern Iran
items.content: Washington announces ‘self-defence’ operation against targets in southern Iran

items.title: Middle East crisis live: US attacks Iran missile sites as Tehran negotiators hold talks in Qatar
items.standfirst: US Central Command claims targets also included boats trying to lay mines, rattling ceasefire, while Iranians meet with Qatari prime minister in DohaIsrael escalates strikes in Lebanon as Netanyahu vows to ‘crush’ HezbollahIn Doha, the Qatari talks with Iran’s top negotiator and its foreign minister focused on the strait of Hormuz and Iran’s stockpile of highly enriched uranium, Reuters quoted an official as saying.Iran’s central bank governor also attended to discuss the potential release of frozen Iranian funds as part of a final deal, said the unnamed official, who the news agency said was briefed on the trip.The invocation of the Abraham Accords at this stage gives an altogether new dimension to the diplomatic and mediatory processes because this issue was not on the agenda.” Continue reading...
items.content: US Central Command claims targets also included boats trying to lay mines, rattling ceasefire, while Iranians meet with Qatari prime minister in DohaIsrael escalates strikes in Lebanon as Netanyahu vows to ‘crush’ HezbollahIn Doha, the Qatari talks with Iran’s top negotiator and its foreign minister focused on the strait of Hormuz and Iran’s stockpile of highly enriched uranium, Reuters quoted an official as saying.Iran’s central bank governor also attended to discuss the potential release of frozen Iranian funds as part of a final deal, said the unnamed official, who the news agency said was briefed on the trip.The invocation of the Abraham Accords at this stage gives an altogether new dimension to the diplomatic and mediatory processes because this issue was not on the agenda.” Continue reading...

items.title: US launches new strikes on Iran, targeting missile sites and boats
items.standfirst: US Central Command says the strikes were taken in "self-defense". It comes as senior Iranian negotiators are in Qatar for talks to end the war.
items.content: US Central Command says the strikes were taken in "self-defense". It comes as senior Iranian negotiators are in Qatar for talks to end the war.

items.title: US strikes southern Iran in ‘self defence’ targeting Iranian mine boats
items.standfirst: The US military says it has launched new strikes on southern Iran, targeting Iranian missile sites and boats.
items.content: The US military says it has launched new strikes on southern Iran, targeting Iranian missile sites and boats.

items.title: US strikes Iran missile sites and mine laying vessels as Trump’s promised peace deal remains elusive
items.standfirst: Negotiators from Iran travelled to Qatar on Monday, with the fate of the country’s nuclear programme and access to frozen assets under discussionMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe US has launched strikes on southern Iran in a test of the seven-week long ceasefire, as both sides played down hopes for an imminent peace deal even as negotiators from Tehran began new talks in Qatar.US forces targeted missile launch sites and boats attempting to lay mines, US central command (Centcom) said on Tuesday, but stressed that the strikes did not indicate the ceasefire with Iran was over. Continue reading...
items.content: Negotiators from Iran travelled to Qatar on Monday, with the fate of the country’s nuclear programme and access to frozen assets under discussionMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe US has launched strikes on southern Iran in a test of the seven-week long ceasefire, as both sides played down hopes for an imminent peace deal even as negotiators from Tehran began new talks in Qatar.US forces targeted missile launch sites and boats attempting to lay mines, US central command (Centcom) said on Tuesday, but stressed that the strikes did not indicate the ceasefire with Iran was over. Continue reading...

items.title: Middle East live: US targets Iranian vessels, missile sites in new strikes
items.standfirst: US forces struck missile sites and vessels allegedly laying naval mines in southern Iran late on Monday, the United States Central Command said. The strikes, which the Pentagon said were taken "in self-defence", threaten to further destabilise a fragile ceasefire as Iranian negotiators are in Doha for high-stakes talks aimed at ending the war. Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.
items.content: US forces struck missile sites and vessels allegedly laying naval mines in southern Iran late on Monday, the United States Central Command said. The strikes, which the Pentagon said were taken "in self-defence", threaten to further destabilise a fragile ceasefire as Iranian negotiators are in Doha for high-stakes talks aimed at ending the war. Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.

items.title: Israel escalates strikes in Lebanon as Netanyahu vows to ‘crush’ Hezbollah
items.standfirst: Hezbollah and Israel launch attacks amid increasingly imperilled ceasefire and stalling talks between US and IranMiddle East crisis: live updatesThe Israeli army has intensified strikes in southern Lebanon, as prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he had ordered the military to escalate its offensive in an effort to “crush” Hezbollah in a further erosion of an already fragmented ceasefire.In turn, Hezbollah said it staged several attacks on Monday on three barracks and a military post in northern Israel “in response to the violation of the ceasefire” by Israel. Continue reading...
items.content: Hezbollah and Israel launch attacks amid increasingly imperilled ceasefire and stalling talks between US and IranMiddle East crisis: live updatesThe Israeli army has intensified strikes in southern Lebanon, as prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he had ordered the military to escalate its offensive in an effort to “crush” Hezbollah in a further erosion of an already fragmented ceasefire.In turn, Hezbollah said it staged several attacks on Monday on three barracks and a military post in northern Israel “in response to the violation of the ceasefire” by Israel. Continue reading...

items.title: Israel to intensify Lebanon offensive to ‘crush’ Hezbollah
items.standfirst: Prime Minister Netanyahu's order comes despite 'ceasefire' agreed with Lebanon last month, which was recently extended.
items.content: Prime Minister Netanyahu's order comes despite 'ceasefire' agreed with Lebanon last month, which was recently extended.

items.title: Israel pounds Lebanon with fresh air strikes, vows to 'crush' Hezbollah
items.standfirst: Israel intensified air strikes across southern and eastern Lebanon on Monday after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed to “crush” Hezbollah, deepening fears of a wider regional conflict. The escalation comes despite an April ceasefire and as another round of US-Iran talks takes place in Doha in a bid to negotiate terms for a broader Middle East peace deal.
items.content: Israel intensified air strikes across southern and eastern Lebanon on Monday after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed to “crush” Hezbollah, deepening fears of a wider regional conflict. The escalation comes despite an April ceasefire and as another round of US-Iran talks takes place in Doha in a bid to negotiate terms for a broader Middle East peace deal.

Reported by Sky News World, CBS News, Reuters Top News and others

items.title: Pope Leo warns about AI and calls for regulation as he quotes from the Lord of the Rings

items.title: Pope Says AI Should Be Disarmed to Avoid Dominating Humanity
items.standfirst: Pope Leo XIV said artificial intelligence should be “disarmed” to protect humanity from its dangers, adding his voice to a heated debate over the extent to which governments should regulate a technology that is reshaping the world.
items.content: Pope Leo XIV said artificial intelligence should be “disarmed” to protect humanity from its dangers, adding his voice to a heated debate over the extent to which governments should regulate a technology that is reshaping the world.

items.title: Sonny Rollins, trailblazing jazz saxophonist, dies at age 95
items.standfirst: Sonny Rollins, the legendary tenor saxophonist known for his bold tone and constant experimentation, has died at 95.
items.content: Sonny Rollins, the legendary tenor saxophonist known for his bold tone and constant experimentation, has died at 95.

items.title: Sonny Rollins, towering jazz innovator known as 'Saxophone Colossus,' dies at 95
items.standfirst: Sonny Rollins, the fiercely inventive tenor saxophonist whose decades-spanning career helped define modern jazz and earned him the nickname 'Saxophone Colossus,' died Monday at his home in upstate New York, according to a statement posted to his social media accounts. He was 95.
items.content: Sonny Rollins, the fiercely inventive tenor saxophonist whose decades-spanning career helped define modern jazz and earned him the nickname 'Saxophone Colossus,' died Monday at his home in upstate New York, according to a statement posted to his social media accounts. He was 95.

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: Rescuers race to free seven people trapped in flooded Laos cave
items.standfirst: A team of experts who helped free a teen football team from a Thai cave in 2018 are among the rescuers.
items.content: A team of experts who helped free a teen football team from a Thai cave in 2018 are among the rescuers.

items.title: Thai rescuers join effort to free seven people trapped in Laos cave
items.standfirst: Group have been stuck in flooded cave in central Laos for five days after heavy rain caused landslidesDivers who helped in the dramatic rescue of a young Thai football team in 2018 have joined efforts to free seven people who have been trapped for five days inside a remote, flooded cave in central Laos.The group entered the cave in Xaysomboun province on Wednesday to hunt for wildlife and search for gold, reports suggest. Heavy rain led to landslides, which blocked the cave entrance. Continue reading...
items.content: Group have been stuck in flooded cave in central Laos for five days after heavy rain caused landslidesDivers who helped in the dramatic rescue of a young Thai football team in 2018 have joined efforts to free seven people who have been trapped for five days inside a remote, flooded cave in central Laos.The group entered the cave in Xaysomboun province on Wednesday to hunt for wildlife and search for gold, reports suggest. Heavy rain led to landslides, which blocked the cave entrance. Continue reading...

items.title: Experts from Thai rescue join efforts to save 7 trapped in Laos cave
items.standfirst: Seven Laotian villagers entered a cave five days ago searching for gold and were trapped inside after heavy rain triggered flash flooding.
items.content: Seven Laotian villagers entered a cave five days ago searching for gold and were trapped inside after heavy rain triggered flash flooding.

clusters.title: Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO
clusters.neutral_headline: Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO
bullet_summary: Uganda has confirmed two new Ebola cases among healthcare workers in Kampala, bringing the country’s total to seven, all linked to the outbreak in neighbouring Democratic Republic of Congo
The DRC’s ministry of communication said in a post on X on Sunday that there were 904 suspected cases and 119 suspected deaths

items.title: Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO
items.standfirst: Director general of World Health Organization urges neighbouring countries to take immediate actionThe World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak is outpacing response efforts and countries neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) are at high risk from the disease.“We are urgently scaling up operations, but at the moment the epidemic is outpacing us,” said the WHO’s director-general, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, as he urged neighbouring countries to take immediate action. Continue reading...
items.content: Director general of World Health Organization urges neighbouring countries to take immediate actionThe World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak is outpacing response efforts and countries neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) are at high risk from the disease.“We are urgently scaling up operations, but at the moment the epidemic is outpacing us,” said the WHO’s director-general, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, as he urged neighbouring countries to take immediate action. Continue reading...

items.title: DR Congo: Authorities struggle to contain fast-moving Ebola outbreak
items.standfirst: Uganda has confirmed two new Ebola cases among healthcare workers in Kampala, bringing the country’s total to seven, all linked to the outbreak in neighbouring Democratic Republic of Congo. In eastern Congo, where more than 900 suspected cases have been identified, authorities and volunteers are struggling to contain the spread amid limited resources, ongoing violence, and the absence of a vaccine or treatment for the rare Bundibugyo strain.
items.content: Uganda has confirmed two new Ebola cases among healthcare workers in Kampala, bringing the country’s total to seven, all linked to the outbreak in neighbouring Democratic Republic of Congo. In eastern Congo, where more than 900 suspected cases have been identified, authorities and volunteers are struggling to contain the spread amid limited resources, ongoing violence, and the absence of a vaccine or treatment for the rare Bundibugyo strain.

items.title: WHO puts suspected Ebola deaths at 220, warns outbreak is outpacing response efforts
items.standfirst: The World Health Organization warned Monday that a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo and neighbouring Uganda was outpacing response efforts, with at least 220 suspected deaths reported. Health workers in the conflict-hit Ituri province face attacks on medical facilities, complicating efforts to contain the rare Bundibugyo strain of the virus.
items.content: The World Health Organization warned Monday that a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo and neighbouring Uganda was outpacing response efforts, with at least 220 suspected deaths reported. Health workers in the conflict-hit Ituri province face attacks on medical facilities, complicating efforts to contain the rare Bundibugyo strain of the virus.

items.title: Suspected Ebola cases in DR Congo outbreak jump to more than 900
items.standfirst: The number of suspected Ebola cases has jumped to more than 900 in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), where authorities are struggling to contain an outbreak.
items.content: The number of suspected Ebola cases has jumped to more than 900 in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), where authorities are struggling to contain an outbreak.

items.title: Suspected Ebola cases in DRC pass 900 as health workers face attacks and shortages
items.standfirst: WHO says outbreak poses ‘very high’ risk for country, but risk of disease spreading globally remains lowAuthorities in the Democratic Republic of the Congo say that suspected Ebola cases have now passed 900 in the ongoing outbreak in the east of the country.The DRC’s ministry of communication said in a post on X on Sunday that there were 904 suspected cases and 119 suspected deaths. Continue reading...
items.content: WHO says outbreak poses ‘very high’ risk for country, but risk of disease spreading globally remains lowAuthorities in the Democratic Republic of the Congo say that suspected Ebola cases have now passed 900 in the ongoing outbreak in the east of the country.The DRC’s ministry of communication said in a post on X on Sunday that there were 904 suspected cases and 119 suspected deaths. Continue reading...

clusters.neutral_headline: Threat of massive blast eliminated for California chemical leak, officials say
bullet_summary: California crews conducted an overnight operation at the Orange County site of a chemical leak in Garden Grove and said Monday that the threat of a massive explosion had been "eliminated
Reported by CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: Threat of massive blast eliminated for California chemical leak, officials say
items.standfirst: California crews conducted an overnight operation at the Orange County site of a chemical leak in Garden Grove and said Monday that the threat of a massive explosion had been "eliminated."
items.content: California crews conducted an overnight operation at the Orange County site of a chemical leak in Garden Grove and said Monday that the threat of a massive explosion had been "eliminated."

items.title: Risk of catastrophic blast from California chemical leak eliminated, but other concerns remain
items.standfirst: The threat of a catastrophic explosion from a chemical vapor leak in Orange County, California, was ruled out Monday after officials discovered a crack in a failing tank at GKN Aerospace Saturday night. Concerns of a smaller explosion still remain. Jonathan Vigliotti reports.
items.content: The threat of a catastrophic explosion from a chemical vapor leak in Orange County, California, was ruled out Monday after officials discovered a crack in a failing tank at GKN Aerospace Saturday night. Concerns of a smaller explosion still remain. Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

items.title: Explosion threat at southern California chemical tank eliminated, firefighters say
items.standfirst: US officials credit overnight cooling and monitoring for stabilising tank after leak fears triggered mass evacuationFirefighters contending with an overheating tank of hazardous chemicals in southern California said they had eliminated the threat of the tank exploding in an overnight operation, Orange county fire officials said on Monday.The damaged chemical tank in southern California had cracked over the weekend, which authorities were hopeful would relieve pressure and reduce the risk of an explosion. Continue reading...
items.content: US officials credit overnight cooling and monitoring for stabilising tank after leak fears triggered mass evacuationFirefighters contending with an overheating tank of hazardous chemicals in southern California said they had eliminated the threat of the tank exploding in an overnight operation, Orange county fire officials said on Monday.The damaged chemical tank in southern California had cracked over the weekend, which authorities were hopeful would relieve pressure and reduce the risk of an explosion. Continue reading...

items.title: Threat of massive blast eliminated for California chemical leak, officials say
items.standfirst: More than 50,000 people in California remain evacuated due to an Orange County chemical leak that prompted Gov. Newsom to request that President Trump issue a Federal Emergency Declaration.
items.content: More than 50,000 people in California remain evacuated due to an Orange County chemical leak that prompted Gov. Newsom to request that President Trump issue a Federal Emergency Declaration.

items.title: Trial of Jeffrey Donaldson for alleged sex offences to begin in Newry
items.standfirst: Former DUP leader faces charges spanning 21 years in case that triggered political earthquake in Northern IrelandThe trial of former Democratic Unionist party (DUP) leader Sir Jeffrey Donaldson for alleged sex offences is set to begin in a case that triggered a political earthquake in Northern Ireland.Donaldson, 63, is charged with rape, gross indecency and other sexual offences spanning 21 years. His wife, Eleanor Donaldson, 60, is charged with aiding and abetting rape and indecent assault and will be subject to a trial of facts. Continue reading...
items.content: Former DUP leader faces charges spanning 21 years in case that triggered political earthquake in Northern IrelandThe trial of former Democratic Unionist party (DUP) leader Sir Jeffrey Donaldson for alleged sex offences is set to begin in a case that triggered a political earthquake in Northern Ireland.Donaldson, 63, is charged with rape, gross indecency and other sexual offences spanning 21 years. His wife, Eleanor Donaldson, 60, is charged with aiding and abetting rape and indecent assault and will be subject to a trial of facts. Continue reading...

items.title: Donaldson sex abuse trial to begin with jury selection
items.standfirst: Former DUP leader Sir Jeffrey Donaldson has pleaded not guilty to all 18 charges he faces, including one count of rape.
items.content: Former DUP leader Sir Jeffrey Donaldson has pleaded not guilty to all 18 charges he faces, including one count of rape.

clusters.title: Iran national football team to stay in Mexico during 2026 World Cup
clusters.neutral_headline: Iran national football team to stay in Mexico during 2026 World Cup
bullet_summary: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that "We have no reason to deny them the possibility of staying in Mexico

items.title: Iran national football team to stay in Mexico during 2026 World Cup
items.standfirst: Iran's national football team will base itself in Mexico during the 2026 FIFA World Cup and travel into the United States only on matchdays, after Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Washington did not want the team staying in the country throughout the tournament.
items.content: Iran's national football team will base itself in Mexico during the 2026 FIFA World Cup and travel into the United States only on matchdays, after Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Washington did not want the team staying in the country throughout the tournament.

items.title: President Sheinbaum allows Iran team to stay in Mexico during World Cup after US refusal
items.standfirst: Fifa approached Mexico after US declined to host Iran squad despite it playing group games in the United StatesMexico’s president, Claudia Sheinbaum, said on Monday her government agreed to allow the Iranian national football team to stay in Mexico during the World Cup, adding that the US did not want to host the team.The team will still play its group stage matches in the US but its base has been moved to Tijuana, Mexico, just south of San Diego, California, a move that Iran’s football federation announced recently and that was formally confirmed by Fifa, the sport’s governing body, on Monday. Continue reading...
items.content: Fifa approached Mexico after US declined to host Iran squad despite it playing group games in the United StatesMexico’s president, Claudia Sheinbaum, said on Monday her government agreed to allow the Iranian national football team to stay in Mexico during the World Cup, adding that the US did not want to host the team.The team will still play its group stage matches in the US but its base has been moved to Tijuana, Mexico, just south of San Diego, California, a move that Iran’s football federation announced recently and that was formally confirmed by Fifa, the sport’s governing body, on Monday. Continue reading...

items.title: Mexico to host Iran's national World Cup team after US refusal
items.standfirst: Iran's national football team will base itself in Mexico during the 2026 FIFA World Cup and travel into the United States only on matchdays after US President Trump said they were welcome to participate but that it is better they not stay in the country "for their own life and safety". Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that "We have no reason to deny them the possibility of staying in Mexico."
items.content: Iran's national football team will base itself in Mexico during the 2026 FIFA World Cup and travel into the United States only on matchdays after US President Trump said they were welcome to participate but that it is better they not stay in the country "for their own life and safety". Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that "We have no reason to deny them the possibility of staying in Mexico."

clusters.title: Shooting near White House raises security concerns ahead of America's 250th birthday celebrations
clusters.neutral_headline: White House gunman had previous run-ins with Secret Service, court documents show
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, BBC World

items.title: Shooting near White House raises security concerns ahead of America's 250th birthday celebrations
items.standfirst: Saturday's shooting near the White House has raised security concerns ahead of summer celebrations to mark America's 250th birthday. The shooter, who was killed after opening fire on a Secret Service checkpoint, had previously blocked a White House entry lane last June, court records show. Nicole Sganga reports.
items.content: Saturday's shooting near the White House has raised security concerns ahead of summer celebrations to mark America's 250th birthday. The shooter, who was killed after opening fire on a Secret Service checkpoint, had previously blocked a White House entry lane last June, court records show. Nicole Sganga reports.

items.title: White House gunman had previous run-ins with Secret Service, court documents show
items.standfirst: The suspect previously obstructed a White House entry lane in June of 2025 and told Secret Service agents he was Jesus Christ.
items.content: The suspect previously obstructed a White House entry lane in June of 2025 and told Secret Service agents he was Jesus Christ.

items.standfirst: At least 82 people have been killed and two are missing after a gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China on Sunday, officials say.
items.content: At least 82 people have been killed and two are missing after a gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China on Sunday, officials say.

items.title: Authorities investigate safety lapses after China coal mine blast kills at least 82
items.standfirst: Authorities in northern China were investigating a coal mine operator with a focus on safety lapses as rescuers searched for those missing in the country’s deadliest coal mine explosion in recent years, which killed at least 82 people.
items.content: Authorities in northern China were investigating a coal mine operator with a focus on safety lapses as rescuers searched for those missing in the country’s deadliest coal mine explosion in recent years, which killed at least 82 people.
